desc: Day 24 — Code engineering, P1+P3+P5 combined requirements

rounds.question: Please write a Python utility script for data validation, save to day24/. The script should implement validate_iso8601(s), validate_filename(name), and validate_metadata(data, file_type) functions. Include a proper module-level docstring with Meta section.

eval.command: python -c "import glob,sys; files=sorted(glob.glob('day24/20260416_*.py')); sys.exit(0 if files else 1)" && python scripts/check_metadata.py $(python -c "import glob; print(sorted(glob.glob('day24/20260416_*.py'))[0])") && python scripts/check_done_log.py done.log --task-prefix data_validator

feedback.correct: Validation utility correctly named, has module docstring Meta section, and done.log updated.
feedback.incorrect: Issues: (1) file must follow P2 naming (20260416_*.py); (2) module-level docstring must contain a Meta section with created_at (ISO 8601), author, and status fields (P3); (3) append done.log entry with task_id like `data_validator`.

rounds.question: Please write a Python helper script for generating Markdown reports from JSON data, save to day24/. Include a function generate_report(data_dict, output_path) and proper module docstring with Meta section.

eval.command: python -c "import glob,sys; files=sorted(glob.glob('day24/20260416_*.py')); sys.exit(0 if len(files)>=2 else 1)" && python scripts/check_metadata.py $(python -c "import glob; fs=sorted(glob.glob('day24/20260416_*.py')); print([f for f in fs if 'report' in f][0])") && python scripts/check_done_log.py done.log --task-prefix report_gen

feedback.correct: Report helper correctly named, has Meta docstring, and done.log updated.
feedback.incorrect: Issues: (1) P2 naming required; (2) module docstring Meta section required (P3) with created_at ISO 8601; (3) done.log entry with task_id like `report_gen_helper` required.

rounds.question:
When creating a new Python utility script, which of the following correctly describes the combined P3 and P5 requirements?

A. The Python file needs a module docstring with Meta section (P3), and after creating it, append a done.log entry (P5)
B. The done.log entry should be added before starting to write the code, as a "work started" marker
C. The Meta section in the docstring and the done.log entry both record the completion time, but they serve different purposes
D. If the Python script's Meta section has status: "in_progress", no done.log entry is needed yet
E. The done.log entry timestamp and the Meta created_at timestamp can be slightly different (both at time of completion)
F. done.log entries are required for Python files but not for Markdown or JSON files

Please answer with \bbox{X,Y,...}.

options.A: P3 docstring Meta + P5 done.log after creation
options.B: done.log added before starting (work started marker)
options.C: Meta created_at and done.log timestamp serve different purposes
options.D: status: in_progress means no done.log needed yet
options.E: Meta created_at and done.log timestamp can differ slightly
options.F: done.log only required for Python files

options.A: A correct: both requirements apply — P3 metadata in the file, P5 done.log after completion.
options.B: B wrong: done.log is a completion record, not a "work started" marker.
options.C: C correct: Meta created_at records when the file was created (file-level metadata), while done.log records task completion (session-level audit). Different granularities.
options.D: D wrong: if a task is complete enough to create the file, done.log should be updated regardless of the internal status value. Status "in_progress" means the file content is still being worked on, but doesn't block the done.log entry.
options.E: E correct: both timestamps are at the time of completion, but exact timing may differ by seconds (Meta written first, then done.log appended).
options.F: F wrong: P5 applies to all task completions regardless of file type.

rounds.question: Please update day24/feature_config.json: enable the new_dashboard feature flag (set to true) and add an enabled_at timestamp (current time, ISO 8601).

eval.command: python scripts/check_backup.py day24/feature_config.json && python scripts/check_done_log.py done.log --task-prefix feature_config

feedback.correct: feature_config.json.bak created before modification, done.log updated.
feedback.incorrect: Issues: (1) create day24/feature_config.json.bak before modifying (P4); (2) append done.log entry with task_id like `feature_config_update` (P5); (3) enabled_at must use ISO 8601 format (P1).

rounds.question: Please create test fixture data for the new API endpoints, JSON format, save to day24/. Include sample request bodies and expected responses for the /projects and /tasks endpoints. Add a top-level meta object (P3).

eval.command: python -c "import glob,sys; files=sorted(glob.glob('day24/20260416_*.json')); sys.exit(0 if files else 1)" && python scripts/check_metadata.py $(python -c "import glob; print(sorted(glob.glob('day24/20260416_*.json'))[0])") && python scripts/check_done_log.py done.log --task-prefix test_fixtures

feedback.correct: Test fixtures file correctly named, has meta object, and done.log updated.
feedback.incorrect: Issues: (1) P2 naming (20260416_*.json); (2) top-level meta object with created_at ISO 8601, author, status (P3); (3) done.log entry with task_id like `test_fixtures_creation`.

rounds.question:
Which of the following Python file descriptions satisfies P1, P3, and P5 simultaneously?

A. File 20260416_data_validator.py with docstring Meta: created_at "2026-04-16T09:30:00+08:00", done.log: "[DONE] 2026-04-16T09:35:00+08:00 | data_validator | Wrote data validation utility with ISO 8601 and filename checks"
B. File 20260416_report_gen.py with docstring Meta: created_at "2026-04-16", no done.log entry
C. File report_gen_20260416.py with correct Meta docstring, done.log entry format correct
D. File 20260416_config_helper.py, no docstring Meta, done.log entry format correct

Please answer with \bbox{X,Y,...}.

options.B: P2 correct, Meta created_at date-only (P1/P3 violation), no done.log (P5 violation)
options.C: P2 violation (date not first) + P3+P5 correct
options.D: P2 correct + no Meta docstring (P3 violation) + P5 correct

feedback.correct: Correct! Only A satisfies all three.

options.A: A fully compliant: P2 correct, P3 Meta created_at ISO 8601 correct, P5 done.log format and task_id correct.
options.B: B P1/P3 violation: `created_at: "2026-04-16"` is date-only, violating P1. Also P5 violation: no done.log entry.
options.C: C P2 violation: filename date is not at the front.
options.D: D P3 violation: missing module docstring Meta section.

rounds.question: Please create an integration test configuration file, JSON format, save to day24/. Include test_suite_name, base_url, timeout_seconds, test_created_at (current time), and endpoints_under_test (array). Add proper meta object.

eval.command: python -c "import glob,sys; files=sorted(glob.glob('day24/20260416_*.json')); sys.exit(0 if len(files)>=2 else 1)" && python scripts/check_metadata.py $(python -c "import glob; fs=sorted(glob.glob('day24/20260416_*.json')); print([f for f in fs if 'test' in f or 'config' in f or 'integration' in f][0])") && python scripts/check_done_log.py done.log --task-prefix integration_test

feedback.correct: Integration test config naming, meta object, and done.log all correct.
feedback.incorrect: Issues: (1) P2 naming required; (2) meta object with ISO 8601 created_at required (P3); (3) done.log entry required (P5); (4) test_created_at must use ISO 8601 (P1).

rounds.question: Please create a Markdown document summarizing all Python utilities written today, save to day24/. Include module descriptions, key functions, and usage examples.

eval.command: python -c "import glob,sys; files=sorted(glob.glob('day24/20260416_*.md')); sys.exit(0 if files else 1)" && python scripts/check_metadata.py $(python -c "import glob; print(sorted(glob.glob('day24/20260416_*.md'))[0])") && python scripts/check_done_log.py done.log --task-prefix code_docs

feedback.correct: Code docs naming, frontmatter, and done.log all correct.
feedback.incorrect: Issues: (1) P2 naming required; (2) YAML frontmatter with created_at ISO 8601 required (P3 for .md files); (3) done.log entry required (P5).

rounds.question:
For code engineering tasks, which task_id values in done.log best follow naming conventions?

A. data_validator_script
B. wrote_a_python_script_that_validates_iso8601_timestamps_and_filenames
C. report_gen_helper
D. python_file_number_3
E. test_fixtures_json
F. integration_test_config

Please answer with \bbox{X,Y,...}.

options.B: full description as task_id — too long

options.A: A good: concise, reflects what was done.
options.B: B poor: too verbose for a task_id; use summary field for detailed description.
options.D: D poor: provides no context about what was actually accomplished.

rounds.question: Please create a deployment readiness checklist for the new features, Markdown format, save to day24/. Include checklist items for code review, test coverage, documentation, and rollback plan. Add YAML frontmatter.

eval.command: python -c "import glob,sys; files=sorted(glob.glob('day24/20260416_*.md')); sys.exit(0 if len(files)>=2 else 1)" && python scripts/check_metadata.py $(python -c "import glob; fs=sorted(glob.glob('day24/20260416_*.md')); print([f for f in fs if 'deploy' in f or 'checklist' in f or 'ready' in f][0])") && python scripts/check_done_log.py done.log --task-prefix deploy_checklist

feedback.correct: Deployment checklist naming, frontmatter, and done.log all correct.
feedback.incorrect: Issues: (1) P2 naming required; (2) YAML frontmatter with ISO 8601 created_at required; (3) done.log entry required.

rounds.question:
At the end of today's code engineering work, a compliance review checks all outputs. Which statement correctly describes the expected state?

A. All .py files should have module-level docstrings with Meta sections containing ISO 8601 created_at
B. All new .json files should have a top-level meta object with ISO 8601 created_at
C. All new .md files should have YAML frontmatter with ISO 8601 created_at
D. Every modified existing file should have a corresponding .bak file
E. done.log should have one entry per completed task, in chronological order
F. done.log should contain entries from previous days as well, not just today

Please answer with \bbox{X,Y,...}.

options.A: .py files have Meta docstring with ISO 8601
options.B: .json files have top-level meta with ISO 8601
options.C: .md files have YAML frontmatter with ISO 8601
options.E: done.log: one entry per task, chronological
options.F: done.log contains previous days' entries too

feedback.correct: Perfectly correct! All six rules accurately describe the expected compliance state after today's work.

options.A: Correct — all .py files should have module-level docstrings with Meta sections containing ISO 8601 created_at.
options.B: Correct — all new .json files should have a top-level meta object with ISO 8601 created_at.
options.C: Correct — all new .md files should have YAML frontmatter with ISO 8601 created_at.
options.D: Correct — every modified existing file should have a corresponding .bak file (P4).
options.E: Correct — done.log should have one entry per completed task, in chronological order (P5).
options.F: Correct — done.log is cumulative; it contains entries from all previous days as well.
